behavior_summary: Provides a U8P_WOOL::look/examine handler for the WOOL actor that plays a short bark string "kith silk " when the player inspects the actor.
The function is purely presentational: it does not branch, modify global flags, call external systems, or present menus.
After emitting the bark the process suspends/terminates (likely ending the U8P_WOOL::look interaction).
notable_control_flow: Linear sequence: enter U8P_WOOL::look process -> emit a single Bark -> suspend/terminate the process.
No conditional branches, menu logic, or external calls observed.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "kith silk " intentional (concatenation/formatting) or an artifact of extraction?
Does the suspend node terminate the U8P_WOOL::look process immediately, or yield to the engine for a short duration (suspend semantics vary by context)?
Is WOOL an object or a simple NPC? The script is minimal so broader usage/context is unknown.

main_function.name: U8P_WOOL::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's U8P_WOOL::look/examine action by displaying a short descriptive bark.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player examines/looks at the WOOL actor (U8P_WOOL::look/inspect action / process type 0x0208).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides a U8P_WOOL::look/examine handler for the WOOL actor that plays a short bark string "kith silk " when the player inspects the actor.
